title: Minimize the admission of containers with capabilities assigned

input_row.title: Minimize the admission of containers with capabilities assigned
input_row.description: Do not generally permit containers with capabilities
input_row.rationale: Containers run with a default set of capabilities as assigned by the Container Runtime. Capabilities are parts of the rights generally granted on a Linux system to the root user. In many cases applications running in containers do not require any capabilities to operate, so from the perspective of the principal of least privilege use of capabilities should be minimized. Impact: Pods with containers require capabilities to operate will not be permitted.
input_row.audit: List the policies in use for each namespace in the cluster, ensure that at least one policy requires that capabilities are dropped by all containers.
input_row.remediation: Review the use of capabilities in applications running on your cluster. Where a namespace contains applications which do not require any Linux capabilities to operate consider adding a policy which forbids the admission of containers which do not drop all capabilities. Default Value: By default, there are no restrictions on the creation of containers with additional capabilities References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/security/pod-security-standards/ 2. https://www.nccgroup.trust/uk/our-research/abusing-privileged-and-unprivileged- linux-containers/
input_row.impact: Pods with containers require capabilities to operate will not be permitted.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_pod_security_capabilities_restricted

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Enumerate all namespaces in the cluster using the Kubernetes API (`kubectl get namespaces`).
- For each namespace, check for the presence of Pod Security Admission (PSA) labels or PodSecurityPolicies (if still in use) that enforce the restriction of Linux capabilities:
  - For PSA: Look for namespaces labeled with `pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce=restricted` or higher, which ensures that pods must drop all capabilities except those explicitly allowed by the restricted policy.
  - For PodSecurityPolicy (if legacy): List all PodSecurityPolicy objects (`kubectl get psp -o yaml`) and check for policies with `spec.requiredDropCapabilities` set to `["ALL"]` or that otherwise restrict capabilities.
- For each policy or admission configuration, confirm that it requires containers to drop all Linux capabilities (i.e., `capabilities.drop: ["ALL"]` or equivalent).
- Validate that at least one such policy is enforced in each namespace, as required by the control.
- Pass Criteria: Every namespace must have an enforced Pod Security Admission policy at the "restricted" level or a PodSecurityPolicy that requires all capabilities to be dropped from containers.
- Example commands:
  - `kubectl get ns --show-labels`
  - `kubectl get psp -o yaml`
  - `kubectl get psp <policy-name> -o yaml | grep requiredDropCapabilities`
  - `kubectl get podsecuritypolicy -o=jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{"\t"}{.spec.requiredDropCapabilities}{"\n"}{end}'`
- Fail if any namespace allows pod admission without a policy requiring all capabilities to be dropped.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.8.0 PDF
